title: Orthopedic Surgeon
description: Orthopedic surgeons are medical doctors who specialize in the diagnosis, treatment, prevention, and rehabilitation of injuries, disorders, and diseases of the musculoskeletal system. This includes bones, joints, ligaments, tendons, muscles, and nerves.
content: Orthopedic surgeons in India perform a wide range of procedures, from joint replacements and fracture repairs to arthroscopic surgeries and spinal fusions. They work in hospitals, clinics, and private practices, often collaborating with other medical professionals like physiotherapists and radiologists. The role demands extensive medical knowledge, surgical skills, and empathy for patients.

financialFeasibility.note: Medical education in India, especially for specialized fields like orthopedic surgery, is expensive. While scholarships and educational loans are available, financial stability significantly eases the path.

keyRoles: research and development in orthopedic techniques

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government and private hospitals, with private hospitals often offering higher salaries and more advanced facilities. Rural postings in government hospitals are common after completing MBBS.

courses: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

relatedCareers.reason: Shares foundational surgical skills and knowledge of human anatomy.
relatedCareers.title: General Surgeon
courses: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

relatedCareers.reason: Works closely with orthopedic surgeons in patient rehabilitation.
relatedCareers.title: Physiotherapist

relatedCareers.reason: Provides diagnostic imaging crucial for orthopedic diagnosis and treatment planning.
relatedCareers.title: Radiologist
courses: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, the number of female orthopedic surgeons is steadily increasing in India.
